info.name: CAB WebSocket & Realtime - Ride Events Test
info.description:
Test WebSocket connections for ride realtime events.

Two systems:
1. ride-service STOMP (port 8089) — Driver GPS location updates
2. notification-service Socket.IO (port 9093) — Ride event notifications

NOTE: Postman supports raw WebSocket but NOT Socket.IO protocol natively.
For Socket.IO testing, use the HTML test page or a Socket.IO client.
For STOMP, use Postman WebSocket with STOMP frames.

item.name: 1. STOMP WebSocket - Driver Location (ride-service:8089)
item.description:
ride-service uses Spring STOMP over WebSocket at /ws/rides.

Connection flow:
1. Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:8089/ws/rides
2. Send STOMP CONNECT frame
3. Send location updates to /app/location.update
4. Subscribe to /topic/driver-location/{driverId} for broadcasts

NOTE: In Postman, create a WebSocket request to ws://localhost:8089/ws/rides/websocket (SockJS transport)

request.description:
STOMP CONNECT frame. After connecting, you'll receive:
CONNECTED
version:1.1
heart-beat:0,0

Then send SUBSCRIBE frame to listen for location broadcasts.
Subscribe to driver location updates. You'll receive LOCATION messages when the driver's GPS position changes.

Send driver GPS coordinates via STOMP.

Payload:
{
  "driverId": "driver-user-id",
  "lat": 10.8221,
  "lng": 106.6885
}

This writes to Redis GEO and publishes Kafka driver.location.updated event.

item.name: 1.4 STOMP Send Multiple GPS Updates (simulated movement)

body.raw:
SEND
destination:/app/location.update
content-type:application/json

{"driverId":"{{driverId}}","lat":10.8230,"lng":106.6890}�

SEND
destination:/app/location.update
content-type:application/json

{"driverId":"{{driverId}}","lat":10.8240,"lng":106.6895}�

SEND
destination:/app/location.update
content-type:application/json

{"driverId":"{{driverId}}","lat":10.8250,"lng":106.6900}�

request.description: Send multiple GPS updates to simulate driver movement. Each update writes to Redis GEO.

item.name: 2. Socket.IO - Ride Notifications (notification-service:9093)
item.description:
notification-service uses Socket.IO (corundum) on port 9093.

Socket.IO protocol is NOT raw WebSocket — it has its own handshake (EIO=4).
Postman WebSocket cannot do Socket.IO handshake natively.

Use one of these approaches:
A) Browser console with Socket.IO client library
B) The HTML test page included in this collection (Section 3)
C) Postman's built-in Socket.IO support (Postman v10.21+)

If using Postman Socket.IO support:
1. Create new request → change method to 'Socket.IO'
2. Enter URL: http://localhost:9093
3. Add query param: userId={your-user-id}
4. Connect and emit/listen events

request.description:
This is a placeholder. Use Socket.IO client or Postman Socket.IO support.

Events to EMIT (Client → Server):
- join_room: send bookingId string
- leave_room: send bookingId string
- send_message: send { bookingId, message, senderId }

Events to LISTEN (Server → Client):
- new_notification: { userId, title, message, type, status, createdAt }
- joined_room_success: { bookingId, status }
- left_room_success: { bookingId, status }
- receive_message: { bookingId, message, senderId }

Connection URL: http://localhost:9093?userId={userId}

Kafka topics that trigger notifications:
- ride.created → 'Đang tìm tài xế...'
- ride.assigned/ride.accepted → 'Đã tìm thấy tài xế!'
- ride.rejected → 'Tài xế đã từ chối. Đang tìm tài xế khác...'
- ride.arrived → 'Tài xế đã đến điểm đón!'
- ride.started → 'Chuyến đi đã bắt đầu.'
- ride.completed → 'Chuyến đi đã hoàn thành.'
- ride.cancelled → 'Chuyến đi đã bị hủy.'
- booking.timeout → 'Không tìm thấy tài xế.'
- payment.completed → 'Thanh toán thành công!'

item.name: 3. Notification REST API (notification-service via gateway)
item.description: Test notification-service REST endpoints through API Gateway.

item.name: 4. E2E Flow - Create Booking + Watch WebSocket
item.description:
Step-by-step flow:
1. Create a booking (this triggers Kafka ride.created)
2. notification-service consumes ride.created → emits Socket.IO 'new_notification'
3. matching-service assigns driver → Kafka ride.assigned
4. notification-service consumes ride.assigned → emits Socket.IO 'new_notification'
5. Driver sends GPS via STOMP WebSocket

For each step, check the Socket.IO client or notification REST API.

exec: console.log('bookingId:', result.id, '- Use this to join_room in Socket.IO');

body.raw:
{
  "pickupLocation": "12 Nguyen Van Bao, Go Vap, HCMC",
  "dropoffLocation": "Tan Son Nhat Airport",
  "customerNote": "WS test booking",
  "pickupCoordinates": { "lat": 10.8221, "lng": 106.6885 },
  "dropoffCoordinates": { "lat": 10.8188, "lng": 106.6619 },
  "vehicleType": "SEDAN",
  "paymentMethod": "CASH",
  "estimatedFare": 75000.00,
  "idempotencyKey": "{{$guid}}"
}

item.name: 4.2 Check Booking Status (poll after Socket.IO notification)

request.description:
Poll booking status. After matching-service assigns a driver:
- Kafka ride.assigned emitted
- notification-service sends Socket.IO 'new_notification' to customer
- booking status changes to ASSIGNED

Expected flow:
CREATED → MATCHING → ASSIGNED → ACCEPTED → PICKUP → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED

Driver arrives at pickup. This triggers:
1. Kafka ride.arrived
2. notification-service → Socket.IO 'new_notification' to customer: 'Tài xế đã đến điểm đón!'
3. Booking status → PICKUP

Driver starts ride. This triggers:
1. Kafka ride.started
2. notification-service → Socket.IO 'new_notification': 'Chuyến đi đã bắt đầu.'
3. Booking status → IN_PROGRESS

Driver completes ride. This triggers:
1. Kafka ride.completed
2. notification-service → Socket.IO 'new_notification': 'Chuyến đi đã hoàn thành.'
3. Booking status → COMPLETED

item.name: 5. Socket.IO Test Script (Browser Console)
item.description:
Copy-paste these scripts into browser console (F12) to test Socket.IO.

Requires: <script src="https://cdn.socket.io/4.7.4/socket.io.min.js"></script>
Or use this in an HTML page.
